Unfortunately, I was unable to find any information about Steffensia verrucosa, which could indicate it may be a rare or obscure plant. However, I was able to find information on Stevia rebaudiana, which I will provide as an alternative.

Stevia rebaudiana, also known as Steffensia or Stevia, is a plant species native to South America that belongs to the Asteraceae family and is commonly known as sweet leaf or sugar leaf. Stevia rebaudiana has been used for centuries to treat various health conditions due to its potential health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, reducing inflammation, and aiding in weight loss. The bioactive constituents of Stevia rebaudiana include steviol glycosides, such as stevioside and rebaudioside A, which are responsible for its sweetness and potential medicinal properties. Stevia rebaudiana is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, as well as in food products such as sugar substitutes and sweeteners.
